review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to RBAC and access control, which are core components of Kubernetes security. The original function names were mostly accurate but could be improved for clarity and specificity. The changes ensure that the function names clearly describe the checks being performed, aligning with the best practices for naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Use 'checked' instead of 'enforced' to better reflect the nature of the function as a check rather than an enforcement action.
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | revocation of access authorizations enforce the revocation
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(8) focuses on the enforcement of access revocation. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that role bindings and service account tokens are properly revoked when access is no longer authorized. This involves checking that role bindings are updated or removed when access needs change, and that service account tokens are invalidated when no longer needed. Additionally, ensuring that token revocation policies are configured can help enforce this control.
